title: Otis and the Mystery of the Disappearing Cookies
synopsis: When cookies keep disappearing from the school cafeteria, young detective Otis uses science and observation skills to solve the mystery with help from his diverse group of friends.

chapters.title: The Case of the Missing Cookies

pages.text: Otis loved cookies. He loved chocolate chip cookies. He loved sugar cookies. He loved oatmeal raisin cookies too. But today, something was very wrong at Sunny Elementary School. "The cookies are gone!" cried Mrs. Chen, the lunch lady. She had curly black hair and wore a bright yellow apron. "I made one hundred cookies this morning. Now there are only ten left!"

illustration.description: A concerned Asian lunch lady with curly black hair and a bright yellow apron stands in a colorful school cafeteria kitchen, pointing at nearly empty cookie trays. Steam rises from ovens in the background with warm orange and yellow lighting.
illustration.alt: Mrs. Chen the lunch lady looking worried in the school kitchen

pages.text: Otis pushed his glasses up his nose. He was a curious boy with brown skin and wild curly hair. "This sounds like a mystery!" he said. His friend Zara nodded. She had long braids and always carried a notebook. "We need to find clues," she said. Their friend Miguel agreed. He had short black hair and loved to help solve problems. "Let's be detectives!" Miguel said with a big smile.

illustration.description: Three diverse children stand together in the cafeteria - Otis, a Black boy with glasses and curly hair looking excited, Zara, a girl with long braids holding a notebook, and Miguel, a Latino boy with short black hair smiling. Bright cafeteria colors surround them.
illustration.alt: Otis, Zara, and Miguel ready to solve the mystery

pages.text: "Where did you last see the cookies?" Otis asked Mrs. Chen. "Right here on these trays," she said. "I put them here after they cooled down." Otis looked around carefully. He saw crumbs on the floor. He saw crumbs on the counter. "Look!" he said. "The crumbs make a trail!" The trail of crumbs led from the kitchen toward the back door.

illustration.description: Otis crouches down examining a trail of cookie crumbs on the checkered cafeteria floor, his finger pointing at the crumbs. Golden sunlight streams through windows, and colorful lunch tables are visible in the background.
illustration.alt: Otis following a trail of cookie crumbs on the floor

pages.text: The three friends followed the crumb trail outside. "The crumbs go to the big oak tree," Zara wrote in her notebook. Under the tree, they found more clues. "Look at these!" Miguel pointed to small paw prints in the dirt. Otis bent down to look closer. "These prints are tiny," he said. "And there are so many of them!"

illustration.description: The three children crouch under a massive oak tree with lush green leaves, examining tiny paw prints in brown dirt. Dappled sunlight filters through the leaves, creating a magical forest atmosphere with rich greens and golden light.
illustration.alt: Children examining paw prints under the oak tree

pages.text: "Wait," said Otis. "I hear something." They all listened carefully. Scratch, scratch, scratch. The sound came from above them. "It's coming from the tree!" whispered Zara. Miguel pointed up. "There!" he said. High up in the oak tree, they could see a small hole. More scratching sounds came from inside the tree trunk.

illustration.description: The children look up at a tall oak tree with expressions of wonder and curiosity, pointing toward a dark hole in the trunk. Warm afternoon light creates dramatic shadows through the leaves, with rich browns and greens dominating the scene.
illustration.alt: Children looking up at a hole in the oak tree trunk

pages.text: "I think I know who took the cookies," said Otis with a grin. Just then, a small gray head poked out of the hole. It was a squirrel! Then another squirrel appeared. And another! Soon, five fluffy squirrels were looking down at them. Each squirrel had cookie crumbs on their whiskers. "The squirrels took the cookies!" laughed Miguel.

illustration.description: Five adorable gray squirrels with big expressive eyes peek out from the tree hole, cookie crumbs visible on their whiskers and paws. The children below point and smile with delight. Warm, cozy colors with soft lighting create a whimsical mood.
illustration.alt: Five squirrels with cookie crumbs peeking out of the tree hole

pages.text: "But how did they get into the kitchen?" asked Zara. Otis thought hard. He remembered something important. "The back door was open this morning," he said. "Mrs. Chen was bringing in milk from the truck." The friends walked back to look at the door. Sure enough, there was a small gap at the bottom. "The squirrels squeezed through here!" said Miguel.

illustration.description: The children examine a small gap under a bright red school door. Otis points at the gap while his friends observe. The scene shows the back entrance with concrete steps and morning shadows, rendered in warm, inviting colors.
illustration.alt: Children discovering the gap under the back door

pages.text: They ran to tell Mrs. Chen what they found. "Squirrels!" she laughed. "Well, that explains everything." Otis had an idea. "We can help you squirrel-proof the kitchen," he said. "How?" asked Mrs. Chen. "Science!" said Otis proudly. "We can put a brush under the door to block the gap. And we can make a special feeder outside so the squirrels have their own food."

illustration.description: In the bright kitchen, Mrs. Chen listens with amusement as Otis enthusiastically explains his solution, gesturing with his hands. Zara takes notes while Miguel nods approvingly. The kitchen has warm yellow walls and cheerful lighting.
illustration.alt: Otis explaining his squirrel-proofing plan to Mrs. Chen

pages.text: The next day, their plan worked perfectly! Mr. Johnson, the janitor, helped them install a brush under the door. He had kind eyes and wore blue overalls. Mrs. Chen put a squirrel feeder by the oak tree. The squirrels were happy with their own food. And the cookies stayed in the kitchen where they belonged. "Great detective work!" said Principal Martinez, who had heard about their mystery-solving adventure. Otis, Zara, and Miguel felt very proud. They had used observation and science to solve their first mystery together!

illustration.description: A celebration scene showing the diverse group - Otis, Zara, Miguel, Mrs. Chen, Mr. Johnson in blue overalls, and Principal Martinez - all smiling proudly in the cafeteria. In the background through the window, happy squirrels can be seen at their new feeder by the oak tree.
illustration.alt: Everyone celebrating the solved mystery with squirrels visible outside at their feeder

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was missing from the school kitchen?
questions.explanation: Mrs. Chen discovered that 90 cookies had disappeared from her kitchen trays.

questions.question: What did Otis find that helped solve the mystery?
questions.explanation: Otis noticed crumbs on the floor that made a trail leading from the kitchen to the back door.

questions.question: Who really took the cookies?
questions.explanation: Five squirrels had been taking the cookies and storing them in their tree hole.

questions.question: How did the cookie thieves get into the kitchen?
choices: Through a gap under the door
questions.explanation: The squirrels squeezed through a small gap under the back door when it was open.

questions.question: What was Otis's solution to prevent more cookie theft?
choices: Put a brush under the door and make a squirrel feeder
questions.explanation: Otis suggested blocking the gap with a brush and giving the squirrels their own food source outside.

pages.title: Complete the Detective Story
pages.instructions: Fill in the missing words to complete the story about Otis solving the mystery.
pages.text: Otis found a trail of ___1___ on the floor. The trail led to the big ___2___ tree outside. Under the tree, they found tiny ___3___ prints in the dirt. The sounds came from a ___4___ in the tree trunk. Five ___5___ had been taking the cookies!

pages.title: Mystery Word Match
pages.instructions: Match each clue word with what the detectives discovered.
pages.text: The young detectives found many ___6___ that helped them solve the mystery. They used ___7___ to figure out how to stop the problem. Working as a ___8___ made them successful.
